STAT_CONC_DEF: Poverty is defined as living on less than $1.90 per day in 2011 PPP terms.
SOURCE_TYPE: Household surveys (HHS, LSMS, DHS), National accounts, Population census
COLL_METHOD: Data collected through national household surveys with standard questionnaires
DATA_SOURCE: World Bank - Poverty and Equity Database
INST_MANDATE: World Bank is responsible for monitoring global poverty indicators as part of its mandate to reduce poverty.
RATIONALE: This indicator measures the proportion of the global population living in extreme poverty, which is essential for tracking progress toward SDG 1.
REC_USE_LIM: Data quality may vary across countries. Some countries have limited survey coverage or outdated surveys.
DATA_COMP: Proportion = (Number of people living on less than $1.90 per day / Total population) × 100
DATA_VALIDATION: Data validated through World Bank quality assurance procedures and peer review
DOC_METHOD: Countries should conduct household surveys at least every 3-5 years with standardized questionnaires
QUALITY_MGMNT: World Bank maintains strict data quality standards and documentation
QUALITY_ASSURE: Multiple quality checks and validations performed on all submitted data
QUALITY_ASSMNT: Data assessed for completeness, accuracy, and consistency with previous years
COVERAGE: Disaggregated by sex, age group, rural/urban, and region where available
COMPARABILITY: Data comparable across time series and countries using PPP methodology
OTHER_DOC: World Bank Poverty and Equity Database: https://data.worldbank.org/poverty
SDG_INDICATOR__GLOBAL: Indicator 18.1.1: Proportion of population below the international poverty line, by sex and age
SDG_TARGET__GLOBAL: Target 18.1: By 2030, eradicate extreme poverty for all people everywhere, currently measured as people living on less than $1.90 a day.
SDG_RELATED_INDICATORS__GLOBAL:
1.2.1 Proportion of population living below the national poverty line

1.2.2 Proportion of men, women and children of all ages living in poverty
SDG_INDICATOR_INFO: Proportion of population below the international poverty line
IND_DEF_CON_CLASS: The proportion of the population below the international poverty line of $1.90 per day.
OTHER_METHOD: Adjustments made for inflation and purchasing power parity (PPP) conversions
